name: John Mayer
description: John Mayer's tone is defined by smooth, dynamic overdrive and lush reverb. His pedalboard has evolved over the years but always centers around transparent drive and expressive dynamics.
famousSongs: Slow Dancing in a Burning Room

pedals.name: Klon Centaur
pedals.note: The original Klon is rare — Soul Food is the closest affordable clone

pedals.name: Ibanez TS10 Tube Screamer
pedals.note: Mayer uses a vintage TS10, the TS9 is nearly identical

pedals.name: Strymon Flint
pedals.note: Tremolo + Reverb combo — RV-6 covers the reverb side

pedals.name: Way Huge Aqua-Puss
pedals.note: Analog delay for slapback — Carbon Copy is a great alternative

pedals.name: Boss TU-3 Tuner
pedals.note: Standard tuner on Mayer's board

name: Jimi Hendrix
description: Hendrix pioneered the use of effects in rock music. His setup was simple but revolutionary — a wah, a fuzz, and a Uni-Vibe were all he needed to change music forever.

pedals.name: Dallas Arbiter Fuzz Face
pedals.note: The FFM3 Mini captures the classic silicon Fuzz Face tone

pedals.name: Vox Wah
pedals.note: Hendrix made the wah pedal famous

pedals.name: Uni-Vibe
pedals.note: Uni-Vibe is a unique effect — Phase 90 gets you in the ballpark

pedals.name: Octavia
pedals.note: Octave-up fuzz effect heard on Purple Haze

name: David Gilmour
description: Gilmour's tone is all about sustain, space, and emotion. His massive pedalboard features multiple delays, a Big Muff for soaring leads, and subtle modulation.

pedals.name: Electro-Harmonix Big Muff Pi
pedals.note: The Big Muff is essential for Gilmour's lead tone

pedals.name: Boss CS-2 Compressor
pedals.note: CS-3 is the modern successor to Gilmour's CS-2

pedals.name: MXR Dyna Comp
pedals.note: Used alongside the Boss compressor

pedals.name: TC Electronic 2290 Delay
pedals.note: Studio-grade delay — Timeline is the modern equivalent

pedals.name: Boss RV-6 Reverb
pedals.note: Lush reverb for ambient passages

pedals.name: MXR Phase 90
pedals.note: Subtle phaser on clean passages

name: The Edge (U2)
description: The Edge built his entire sound around delay. His rhythmic, dotted-eighth-note delays create the shimmering, atmospheric sound that defines U2.
famousSongs: Where the Streets Have No Name

pedals.name: Boss DD-8 Digital Delay
pedals.note: The Edge uses multiple delays — DD series is a staple

pedals.name: Strymon Timeline
pedals.note: For complex delay patterns and presets

pedals.name: Electro-Harmonix POG2
pedals.note: Organ-like tones on songs like 'Mysterious Ways'

pedals.name: Korg SDD-3000
pedals.note: Legendary rack delay — Flashback 2 has an SDD preset

pedals.name: TC Electronic Corona Chorus
pedals.note: Subtle chorus for width

pedals.name: Boss TU-3 Tuner
pedals.note: Standard tuner

name: Kurt Cobain
description: Cobain's setup was deliberately simple and raw. A Boss DS-1 or DS-2 for distortion, a Small Clone for the iconic 'Come As You Are' chorus, and that's about it.

pedals.name: Boss DS-1 Distortion
pedals.note: Cobain's go-to distortion pedal

pedals.name: Boss DS-2 Turbo Distortion
pedals.note: Used later — DS-1 is the closest in our database

pedals.name: Electro-Harmonix Small Clone
pedals.note: The chorus sound on 'Come As You Are'

pedals.name: Tech 21 SansAmp
pedals.note: Used for recording — MT-2 gives similar heavy tones

name: John Frusciante
description: Frusciante's tone blends funk, punk, and psychedelia. His board features a mix of vintage and modern effects with an emphasis on modulation and dynamic playing.

pedals.name: Boss DS-2 Turbo Distortion
pedals.note: His signature distortion — DS-1 is the closest match

pedals.name: Ibanez WH10 Wah
pedals.note: Unique wah tone — Cry Baby is the standard alternative

pedals.name: Boss CE-1 Chorus Ensemble
pedals.note: Vintage chorus — CE-5 is the modern Boss chorus

pedals.name: Electro-Harmonix Big Muff Pi
pedals.note: Used for heavy, sustaining fuzz tones

pedals.name: MXR Micro Amp
pedals.note: Clean boost — Soul Food can serve as a transparent boost
